analyst_briefing: All 10 active conflicts remain at week_count 1 (Contested baselines, null indicator_medians), precluding quantitative deviation scoring and trajectory changes; epistemic uncertainty governs all assessments. CORE PRINCIPLE violation: no baselines means no deviation-over-level early warnings possible this cycle—prioritize data maturation. US-Israel-Iran shows 4x RED-band indicators (I1-5=5) with Hormuz blockade and Apr 25 talks collapse (weekly I5 worsening), but null baseline blocks alert; habituation risk if treated stable. Israel-Lebanon I6=5 (1M displaced) with unraveling ceasefire demands watch. F4 flags maintained across Gaza/DRC/Iran/Lebanon on unverified de-escalation; I5 precision: Iran AMBER (impasse) not RED expulsion. Roster: Include India-Pakistan (Op Sindoor dual I1/I2 met); add Ethiopia to watch. Cross-flags: GMM HIGH on Hormuz/oil disruptions; ERM HIGH Sudan/Lebanon displacement velocity; WDM HIGH Sahel democratic stress. Lead signal (JNIM Bamako) external to roster—recommend Sahel inclusion. Next cycle: lock baselines at week 13; apply q2_2026 recalcs (Sudan/Myanmar/Russia doctrine). Focus: structural REDs in new wars (Iran/Lebanon) over familiar highs.
